articles.title: GOOGLE'S AI MODE GETS BETTER AT UNDERSTANDING VISUAL PROMPTS
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode now better understands visual search prompts through an enhanced "query fan-out" technique, which runs multiple background searches to interpret complex requests, such as "moody but maximalist" bedrooms. The update makes the chatbot multimodal, allowing users to initiate conversations with images or videos and receive more visual responses rather than text-heavy answers. These improvements particularly benefit shopping searches, enabling AI Mode to handle nuanced queries such as "barrel jeans that aren't too baggy" with refinable follow-up questions.

articles.title: APPLE GLASSES ARE COMING: HERE'S EVERY RUMORED NEW FEATURE
articles.summary: Apple is developing AI-driven Apple Glasses expected in 2026, with release likely in 2027. The first version will lack a display or AR features, focusing instead on visual intelligence, Siri voice control, multiple cameras, health tracking, and deep iPhone integration. Powered by a low-power chip and built-in speakers, the glasses will function as an iPhone accessory, with a future model reportedly planned to include a display.

articles.title: META PLANS TO SELL TARGETED ADS BASED ON DATA IN YOUR AI CHATS
articles.summary: Meta will use data from user interactions with its AI products—including Meta AI chatbot conversations, Ray-Ban smart glasses recordings, and AI-generated content—to sell targeted ads on Facebook and Instagram starting December 16. The policy applies globally except in South Korea, the UK, and the EU, with no opt-out available for affected users. While Meta claims conversations about sensitive topics won't influence ads, the company acknowledges that it's still developing systems to leverage this AI data for its advertising business.

articles.title: WHERE'S THE AI DESIGN RENAISSANCE?
articles.summary: Despite 2.5 years of intense AI hype, there's no evidence that current AI tools are making design faster, eliminating designer jobs, or enabling the promised software renaissance—metrics like new app releases and domain registrations remain flat. AI struggles with designs that require tight constraints, novelty, or complexity because its architecture is based on training data, making it best suited for average designs rather than bold, opinionated work that stands out. Designers should focus on out-of-training-data skills, such as pixel-perfect UI and novel interactions, while using AI primarily for small personal projects, internal prototypes, and simple public tools where security and complexity are minimal.

articles.title: BORING IS GOOD
articles.summary: AI language models should focus on "boring" tasks, like proofreading and summarizing, rather than attempting high-level thinking, where hallucinations and faulty reasoning can lead to failures. Small language models (SLMs) are sufficient for these modest language tasks, offering benefits such as easier ethical training and lower costs. The industry has been using LLMs backwards by trying to automate complex tasks from the top down instead of augmenting simpler tasks from the bottom up.

articles.title: HOW DO GOOD DESIGNERS GET STUCK? THEY WASTE TIME TRYING TO MIND-READ THEIR TEAM
articles.summary: Good designers often get stuck by attempting to mind-read their team's intentions when designs are rejected, creating false narratives like "they don't value UX" instead of addressing the real issue. This "LoserThink" pattern prevents designers from asking whether they're solving the actual problem their stakeholders need solved. The solution is to replace assumptions with direct questions about success criteria and understanding team pressures through brief conversations.

articles.title: WAN 2.5 AI VIDEO GENERATOR
articles.summary: Describe what you want to see, and Wan 2.5 will bring it to life with stunning visuals and perfectly synchronized audio.

articles.title: YOUR SPACE FOR NOTES, TASKS, AND BIG IDEAS
articles.summary: Create stunning documents, manage tasks, build dynamic collections, and structure your ideas your way, whether it's personal journals, work projects, or daily planning.

articles.title: DESIGN AND BUILD YOUR WEBSITES WITH AI
articles.summary: Webjourney helps you design, build, and launch stunning websites in minutes by just chatting. No coding required.

articles.title: HOW DESIGN RADICALLY TRANSFORMED A DYING INDUSTRY
articles.summary: Jacek Utko, a former architect turned newspaper art director, transformed struggling newspapers into award-winning successes through bold, user-focused design. Inspired by Cirque du Soleil's creativity, he reimagined newspapers as visual “performance art,” aligning design with business goals and reader experience. His redesigns in Eastern Europe boosted circulation by up to 100%, increased ad revenue, and earned multiple international design awards, proving how strategic design can drive both innovation and measurable business growth.

articles.title: HOW DOUBLE DIAMOND REVIVED A RETRO BRAND WITHOUT IT FEELING DATED
articles.summary: The revival of the classic British beer brand Double Diamond brings nostalgic charm with a modern twist. Originally brewed in 1876 and once a UK best-seller, it faded with changing tastes but has now been relaunched by Jamie Allsopp and Steve Holt using original recipes. Designer Alec Tear and illustrator Satoshi Hashimoto refreshed its iconic logo, typeface, and mascot, blending heritage with contemporary appeal. The result evokes traditional pub culture while feeling bold and relevant to today's drinkers.

articles.title: BRANDON CONSULTANTS DESIGNS NEW PIZZAEXPRESS SPECIALS RANGE
articles.summary: PizzaExpress has launched a new “Specials” range to bring its restaurant experience into homes, responding to the rise of at-home “fakeaways.” Designed by Brandon Consultants, the packaging features real pizzaiolos proudly presenting their creations, emphasizing craftsmanship and human connection while standing out in the crowded chilled aisle. The refreshed design links the new range to the classic line through consistent visual elements, blending PizzaExpress's artistic heritage with a warm, premium feel that evokes its emotional place in British culture.

articles.title: WHY STICKY NAVIGATION CAN UNDERMINE ACCESSIBILITY
articles.summary: Sticky navigation often undermines accessibility by consuming screen space, blocking content, disrupting screen readers, and causing problems when combined with magnification, especially on mobile devices.

articles.title: THE OLDER I GET, THE MORE I REALISE DESIGNING FOR SENIORS IS BETTER FOR YOUNG PEOPLE TOO
articles.summary: Accessible design features meant for seniors, such as larger text, high contrast, bigger buttons, and clear navigation, actually benefit everyone, regardless of age or ability.

articles.title: THIS VIRAL ELEPHANT OPTICAL ILLUSION IS PURE MAGIC
articles.summary: A new viral optical illusion by Science Magician Matt Pritchard features a circle of purple elephants that seem to change brightness as you look at them, demonstrating how color theory and visual perception can trick the eye even without digital effects.
